description: Focused regression deck proving that the leading grid-cell subtitle line renders bold by default on the 2x2 and 2x3 text layouts.

slides.title: Grid templates
slides.subtitle: Regression proof for structural subtitle bolding

slides.title: Four-box text grid
slides.item1:
Control model
One governed workflow keeps product, legal, compliance, and operations aligned inside the same execution lane.
slides.item2:
Delivery speed
Launch teams move faster because approved templates and review gates reduce reinvention across each new product.
slides.item3:
Runtime rules
Transfer, holding, and servicing controls execute systematically instead of depending on manual interpretation.
slides.item4:
Scale potential
The operating model expands across products and jurisdictions without forcing a redesign every time.

slides.title: Six-box text grid
slides.item1:
Templates
Reusable starting points reduce setup time and keep asset launches anchored to approved patterns.
slides.item2:
Policy first
Teams see core constraints before issuance so governance decisions happen earlier in the workflow.
slides.item3:
Identity linked
Verified investor status drives who can hold, receive, or service the instrument in production.
slides.item4:
Event driven
Operational signals keep servicing, settlement, and downstream systems synchronized during runtime.
slides.item5:
Audit evidence
Approvals, policy outcomes, and execution traces remain inspectable for risk and compliance review.
slides.item6:
Native output
Commercial and delivery teams can still edit the slide natively without rebuilding it from scratch.
